# `!user`

**Category:** access

Manage a Linux user account via useradd/usermod/userdel. Linux-only.

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user` | string | yes | Username to manage. |
| `uid` | int | no | Desired numeric UID. |
| `system` | bool | no | Create a system user (useradd -r), only when no uid given. |
| `gid` | string | no | Primary group name or GID. |
| `groups` | list | no | Supplementary group names; compared against id -Gn. |
| `append` | bool | no | When modifying groups, add to existing rather than replacing. |
| `create_home` | bool | no | Create the home directory (-m vs -M). Default true. |
| `create_user_group` | bool | no | Create a group with the same name as the user (-U vs -N). Default true. |
| `gecos` | string | no | GECOS/comment field (-c). |
| `shell` | string | no | Login shell (-s). |
| `remove` | bool | no | Remove the user instead of creating. |
| `cleanup` | bool | no | When removing, also delete the home directory (userdel -r). |

## Examples

```yaml
- !user
  user: deploy
  uid: 2000
  groups:
    - www-data
    - docker
  append: true
  shell: /bin/bash
```
